name: Caught in the Middle | Online
description: Often called the “sandwich generation,” this phase of life sits between responsibility and resilience.If you’re in your 40s or 50s, you probably don’t need an explanation, you are living it. Caring for aging parents while still showing up fully for your children. Holding space for everyone else, while trying to keep your own world from unraveling in the process.It is a lot. And it’s often invisible.This is a space to pause, share, and connect with others who understand the weight of it, without needing to explain it. Come talk to people who get it.

location.address: Delhi, India

name: #lecture27 KUMBH: World’s Largest Gathering
description: #lecture27 #LectureoverdrinksKUMBH﻿World’s largest gathering: What 600 million people can teach us about crowd scienceWhat if the largest, most complex crowd system in the world works without tickets, without tracking, and without central control?At Kumbh Mela, hundreds of millions of people gather over a short span of time. On peak days, tens of millions move through the same space to bathe, eat, rest, and return. There is no RSVP list, no database, no single authority orchestrating movement. And yet, the system holds.In this session, we look at Kumbh not just as a religious gathering, but as a living system. Drawing from his research, Dr Prateek Raj unpacks how crowd flow organises itself, how decentralised systems adapt in real time, and how something he calls the “mandala economy” allows order to emerge from apparent chaos. It shifts the question from how we control large systems to how they might function when control is minimal.The conversation opens up a broader lens on cities, infrastructure, and human coordination. If something at this scale can work without tight oversight, what does that say about the systems we design and the assumptions we carry about order and control?About the SpeakerDr Prateek Raj studies how people and groups at the fringes often drive innovation and social progress. He is the author of Atypical: Five Strategy Rules for a New World and is currently working on a follow-up book on the history of progress.His research spans diverse contexts, including firm productivity in India, elite marriage patterns in South Korea, income gaps among stigmatised business owners, and organisational responses during COVID-19. His recent work on the Kumbh Mela approaches it as a large-scale, self-organising system.Prateek is an Associate Professor at University College London, where he leads the Business and Health programme. He is also affiliated with the Stigler Center and is a Fellow of the Royal Society for Public Health and the Higher Education Academy.Book now & develop your own pint of view! 🍻📌 Note: Alcohol will be served only to guests aged 25 and above. Non-alcoholic options will be available. Attendance implies consent to being filmed for event documentation.

name: Arabic Calligraphy Workshop
description: The art of sacred script, still alive in Old DelhiA chance to witness Arabic Calligraphy not just as writing, but as an art of beauty, balance, and devotion. Rooted in centuries of tradition, it has been used to preserve poetry, language, and sacred texts through flowing strokes and precise forms. Every letter follows a rhythm, every curve has meaning.Traditionally created using reed pens and ink, this practice is as much about patience and discipline as it is about expression. Today, very few traditional practitioners continue to keep it alive.Join Katib Mohammad Ghalib, one of the last traditional calligraphers of Old Delhi, for a hands-on workshop where you will understand the essence of this art and create your own calligraphy piece.Limited seats only.

name: From Plate To Planet
description: Join us for a session that dismantles the idea of health as not a purely individual choice but also reveals it as a biological and communal dialogue shaped by the trillions of microbes living within us.About the LectureHealth is often treated as a matter of personal willpower: something determined solely by the gym or a specific diet. This lecture begins from the opposite premise.Drawing on years of research in metagenomics and microbiome science, this session examines the human body as a complex, plural ecosystem. Focusing on the fascinating "gut-brain axis," the lecture traces how microbial communities behave, communicate, and influence everything from our immune system to our emotional resilience.Moving across themes of dietary shifts, the rise of ultra-processed foods, and approaches to wellbeing, the lecture explores:The Gut-Brain Highway: How the communication network between microbes and the brain shapes our mental landscape.The Standard American Diet (SAD): The impact of modern, low-fiber eating patterns on microbial diversity and rising levels of anxiety.The Inner Ecosystem: Understanding the trillions of microbes that play a silent role in our daily survival.Ubuntu and Interconnectedness: How health grows from the relationship between people, food systems, and the environmentEveryday Stewardship: How small, thoughtful actions can care for both our internal world and the planet.We are "holobionts"—communities of life that require kindness, awareness, and purpose to thrive. It asks how we can reclaim our health by understanding the invisible connections that bind us to our food, our communities, and our world.Flow of the Evening4:00 PM – Doors open: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable.4:05 – 4:20 PM – Place your orders and start a few conversations.4:30 PM – The learning begins.5:15 PM – Open Q&A and discussion with Dr. Brahma.5:30 PM onwards – Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversations.About the SpeakerDr. Vijaya Brahma is a microbiologist and the Director of Ubuntuverse, a community dedicated to living with purpose and kindness. Her scientific journey began with studying microbes during her PhD at CSIR IMTECH and continued through postdoctoral research at the University of Toronto.Today, she works at the intersection of science and everyday life through the Ubuntu Wellness Academy. She is the host of the YouTube channel Shodh Ka Pratibodh, where she humanizes the scientific journey by interviewing researchers about their paths beyond just academic achievements. Much of her work involves mentoring and science communication, turning complex metagenomic research into simple, actionable conversations about the gut microbiome and planetary health.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Nerd Nite Gurgaon
description: Three talks. One night. Drinks encouraged. Nerd Nite is one of the world’s largest knowledge-sharing event series, with chapters in over 100 cities. We are so excited to bring this to Gurgaon.The format is simple: three speakers, each with 15-18 minutes to dive into something they’re nerdy about, followed by a bit of Q&A. The topics can be anything: economics, art, data science, failed utopias, 90s pop culture. If it’s interesting, it’s in.It all started in a Boston bar in 2003, when a biologist got tipsy and gave a talk about birds that commit identity theft. It was ridiculous. And brilliant. Since then, Nerd Nite has grown into a global community of curious people who’d rather spend an evening learning something weird than sitting through a panel or pretending to network. No suits. No panels. No buzzwords. Just smart people, great stories, and a drink in hand.Bring a friend. Bring a question. Bring your nerdiest self. We will bring the rest.

name: Mere Humnafas
description: Mere Humnafas is an intimate, immersive musical–storytelling experience that reconnects contemporary audiences with the emotional and cultural roots of the subcontinent’s rich poetic and musical traditions. At its core lies a simple yet powerful idea: to create something truly new, we must first engage deeply with the masters who shaped our artistic language.For generations, we’ve been reminded that innovation doesn’t exist in isolation—it grows through dialogue with history, memory, and those who came before us. Mere Humnafas brings this philosophy to life by revisiting timeless ghazals, nazms, and poetic expressions that have shaped our collective emotional landscape. These classics are reimagined through contemporary acoustic arrangements, featuring only a keyboard and acoustic guitar, allowing the poetry and emotion to take center stage with striking intimacy.Woven through the music is a narrative that explores enduring themes of love, longing, separation, lost affection, and unspoken devotion—emotions that remain deeply relevant today. Through spoken word and reflection, the experience bridges generations, offering a modern lens while honoring the depth and beauty of the original voices.Mere Humnafas invites you into a dreamy, almost surreal evening—an hour where past and present dissolve into one, where memory and imagination intertwine, and where music and poetry remind us of the power of feeling deeply.This is not just a performance.It is a return—to our roots, our stories, and to each other.

Inspired by the intimate acoustics of traditional havelis, where music once echoed through courtyards and corridors, this concert brings alive a timeless era of expression and artistry.Presented by Vrindan and performed by internationally acclaimed artists, Guzaarish is not just a concert, but a living, breathing exchange between performers and audience. Through a unique request-led format, the evening unfolds organically, allowing listeners to shape the journey and reconnect with melodies that hold personal meaning.Rooted in the golden era of semi-classical music and infused with the charm of vintage cinema, each rendition carries nostalgia, poetic depth and emotional resonance. From beloved classics to hidden gems, Guzaarish invites you to rediscover, reminisce, and revel in a shared musical journey, one that is as intimate as it is unforgettable.

name: Earth & Rhythm: Drum Circle
description: The Earth has its own music… have you been listening?Step into a space where rhythm becomes connection, and sound becomes a shared experience. Rhythm & Earth is not just a drum circle—it’s a guided, immersive journey into collective energy, expression, and presence.In this 60-minute experience, you’ll enter a circle of like-minded individuals and co-create rhythms together—no prior musical experience needed. As the beats align, something shifts: barriers soften, energy opens, and a natural sense of harmony begins to flow.This is where you don’t just play music… you feel it.Each session is intuitively guided yet organically shaped by the group, making every circle a unique and living experience. Whether you come to unwind, connect, or simply explore something new, this is your invitation to drop into rhythm and return to yourself.

location.address: E5, Market Rd, Block E, DLF Phase 1, Sector 26A, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

name: The Morning Table: A Sunday Breakfast Gathering
description: The Brine Club is our way of bringing people together over food that’s meant to be eaten slowly — and building a community of like-minded people around the table.Every table is different, every menu changes — because I like letting the day decide where things go.Most of our gatherings are sit-down.The Morning Table is a Sunday breakfast brunch hosted at our home in Gurgaon. ( Exact location to be shared 24 hours before the event to maintain privacy)We’re putting together a menu of breakfast dishes from different parts of the world — the kind you usually only see online or in other cities. Think London’s viral honey caramel butter toast, fried chicken & waffles, and our own slightly indulgent mango brioche toast, along with a few other plates.One beverage of your choice is included. If you feel like another round, additional drinks can be ordered during the brunch on an à la carte basis.Everything will be served family-style, so the table eats together, passes plates around.The full menu is in the gallery.You’ll be sharing the table with 10 people — some strangers, some soon-to-be friends. If you’re someone who enjoys meeting new, interesting people over really good food, this might be your kind of Sunday morning.It’s relaxed, a little indulgent, and not the kind of breakfast you usually find around the city.

location.address: Gurugram, Haryana, India

name: A summer table from Odisha! - Pakhala Pop Up
description: Pakhala Pop-Up: A Summer Table from OdishaA Rosei Ghara x Kartik’s Kitchen CollaborationWe bring to the table a ritual from coastal Odisha — a meal designed for summer.Pakhala is not just fermented rice. It is comfort, cooling, seasonal wisdom, and memory served in a bowl.On the 26th of April, from 12 pm onwards, Rosei Ghara, in collaboration with Kartik’s Kitchen, invites you to experience an Odia summer table — thoughtfully curated, slow, and rooted in tradition.What to Expect:At the centre sits Pakhala — lightly fermented rice, cooling and probiotic-rich — accompanied by an abundant spread of classic summer sides. Expect crisp fries, smoky mash, leafy greens, lentil badis, seasonal vegetables, and carefully spiced accompaniments — each dish balancing texture, heat, tang, and crunch.Why It’s Special:• A rare opportunity to experience Odisha’s iconic summer meal in Gurgaon• A collaboration between two kitchens that celebrate regional Indian flavours• Limited seating to preserve the intimacy of the table• Hosted in K-38 Studios, DLF PHASE I, Gurgaon• A slow, immersive lunch designed around seasonality and traditionPakhala is typically eaten for lunch in Odia homes — and we invite you to experience it exactly as it is meant to be enjoyed: unhurried, generous, and shared.📍 Venue: K-38 Studios, Gurgaon🗓️ Date : 26th April🕛 Time: 12 pm onwards✨ Limited seats | Community dining experienceCome curious. Come hungry.Leave with a new understanding of how Odisha does summer.

location.address: A 26/13, A Block, DLF Phase 1, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

name: 26th April: Blueprints for Anti-Caste Protagonism
description: Join us for a lecture that asks a difficult question:Why is it so hard to see anti-caste assertion, even when it is right in front of us?About the LectureWhat does it mean to represent a body that history has refused to recognise?This lecture turns to art as a site of both inquiry and resistance, examining how caste operates not only through direct violence, but through subtler, representational forms that shape what can be seen, known, and acknowledged.Drawing from works such as Mukta Salve Triptych (2023) and Tannerfilm (2024), the session traces how infrastructures of caste have historically made it nearly impossible for anti-caste voices and assertions to be positively registered. It examines the lingering continuities between colonial ethnography of the 1850s and contemporary modes of representation — both of which render the Dalit body as devoid of agency, even in moments of clear self-assertion.The talk explores:How caste operates through visual and cultural representationWhy anti-caste assertion is often rendered illegible or erasedThe continuity between colonial and contemporary ways of seeing Dalit subjectivityHow art can function as a space of critique, interruption, and re-imaginationAbout the SpeakerRahee Punyashloka is an artist, writer, and experimental filmmaker based between Bhubaneswar and New Delhi. His work spans visual art, film, and writing, with a focus on creating anti-caste imagery and emancipatory imaginaries for Dalit subjectivity.His works have been exhibited at institutions and festivals including Khoj Studios, the Museum of Art and Photography, Haus der Kulturen der Welt, the Tribeca Film Festival, and the International Film Festival Rotterdam. He is also known by his online moniker artedkar.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue — so you can listen, reflect, and stay for conversation.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who want learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

location.address: Basement, Ground floor and First floor Building no. 12, Panchsheel Community Centre, Shahpur Jat, Panchsheel Park, New Delhi, Delhi 110017, India

name: ALT EFF Film Club x GuruJal Gurugram | 15th May
description: If you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 10 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation.With snacks. Always snacks.PROGRAMME:Some Thoughts on the Common ToadThe FeastThe Butterfly ManThe Floating worldTo become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Film screeningGroup discussionLight refreshmentsCommunity spaceStudent Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFAll Living Things Environmental Film Festival (ALT EFF) is the world’s largest decentralised film festival and a premiere showcase for environmental cinema.Across its annual festival, monthly Film Club, schools initiatives, film fund, and year round curations, ALT EFF nurtures stories grounded in care, collective responsibility, stewardship, and an abiding love for the natural world.Spanning countries, genres, languages, and forms, it brings together stories reimagining our relationship with all living things.

location.address: 6/9, F-2, Block F, DLF Phase 1, Sector 26A, Gurugram, Haryana 122002, India

organizer.name: All Living Things Environmental Film Festival
